pressReleases.title: The Primer Master Plan: Building the First Rental Platform in Northern Nigeria
pressReleases.excerpt: Primer launches with a mission to build the first trusted platform for verified homes, movers, and handyman services in Northern Nigeria—creating a rental experience that is transparent, efficient, and stress-free from start to finish.
pressReleases.fullText:
The Primer Master Plan

Northern Nigeria — February 27, 2026

The Mission

To build the first trusted platform for verified homes, movers, and handyman services in Northern Nigeria—creating a rental experience that is transparent, efficient, and stress-free for everyone.

The Landscape

Southern Nigeria has options. PropertyPro.ng, Nigeria Property Centre, Town NG, Jiji—multiple platforms where people can search for homes.

Northern Nigeria has none.

Students, young professionals, and families across the North navigate the rental market with no verified information. No trusted source for properties. No way to know if a mover is reliable. No directory of handymen who actually show up.

They rely on word of mouth, roadside posters, and hope.

The Opportunity

The first company to build trust in this region doesn't just enter a market—they define it.

Primer will be that company.

Step 1: Create trust from scratch in one community.

We start in one high-density urban area in Northern Nigeria—students, young professionals, people who need homes and services today.

Our team goes out manually. We meet landlords face to face. We photograph every room ourselves. We interview and test every mover and handyman. We build relationships, verify identities, and document everything.

· Properties: We visit every home ourselves. Meet every landlord. Photograph every room. We don't aggregate listings—we create them from scratch.
· Movers: We vet, interview, and test every moving company. Reliability. Pricing transparency. Care with belongings.
· Handymen: We verify skills, background, and pricing. No more 'he came, quoted double, and left.'

When we're done, for the first time in that neighborhood, there will be a small, trusted list of homes and service providers that people can actually rely on.

We're not competing with southern platforms. We're building what doesn't exist here.

Step 2: Scale across the North.

Once the manual model works in one location, we expand to every major city in Northern Nigeria.

We pair geographic expansion with platform features that make renting seamless:

· In-app payments
· Automated contracts
· Service requests
· Transparent ratings
· Verification workflows

But here's what makes Primer different: we don't become a 'listing site.' We become the operating system for renting in the North.

· Tenants find verified homes, book verified movers, and request verified repairs—all in one place.
· Landlords get reliable tenants, vetted service providers, and tools to manage their properties.
· Service providers get consistent work, transparent payments, and reputations they can build.

Step 3: Build the North's first rental ecosystem.

When thousands of Northern Nigerians trust Primer for their homes and services, we become the infrastructure of renting in the region:

· Digital Tenant Identity: A portable profile with verified rental history. Landlords across the North see this and trust tenants—no more exploitative '2 years upfront' demands.
· Landlord OS: Simple tools for property management, tenant screening, and maintenance—built specifically for Northern property owners.
· Service Provider Network: The North's first and largest network of trusted movers and handymen. When something breaks, Northern Nigerians open Primer.
· The Northern Nigeria Rental Registry: Accurate data on housing prices, ownership, and scam patterns—creating transparency where none existed before.

The Reality

We are first in the North. That means:

· Step 1 will be manual. Slow. Unsexy. We will verify every property and every service provider ourselves because no one else has.
· Step 2 will require operational excellence. Scaling trust across multiple cities is harder than scaling code.
· Step 3 is years away. But if we execute Steps 1 and 2 with integrity, no one can follow us there.

We're not building for quick growth. We're building the foundation of rental trust in Northern Nigeria.

From scratch.

Join Us

If you want to build something that doesn't exist yet:

· Verifiers who can hit the streets, meet landlords, and inspect properties across the North.
· Engineers who want to build the first real rental platform in Northern Nigeria.
· Service providers—movers, handymen, repair experts—who want to be part of the first trusted network.
· Believers who understand that first movers who build trust win everything.

Primer is the first step.

Let's build the North.

pressReleases.title: The Origin Story: Why Alamin Ahmed Is Building Primer
pressReleases.excerpt: Founder Alamin Ahmed shares why he started Primer—the first trusted platform for verified homes, movers, and handyman services in Northern Nigeria.
pressReleases.fullText:
The Origin Story

By Alamin Ahmed, Founder of Primer

February 27, 2026

The rental market in Northern Nigeria is invisible.

There are no platforms. No directories. No verified listings. If you're a student, a young professional, or a family looking for a home in the North, your options are simple: walk the streets, call numbers on posters, or ask someone who knows someone.

That's not a market. It's a gap.

And gaps don't close themselves.

The Observation

Southern Nigeria has multiple property platforms. PropertyPro.ng, Nigeria Property Centre, Town NG, Jiji—they've been around for years. Thousands of listings. Millions of users.

The North has none.

Not one.

Not for lack of people needing homes. Not for lack of landlords with properties to rent. Not for lack of movers and handymen looking for work.

The North has none because building trust is hard. It requires feet on the ground. Relationships. Verification that can't be automated away.

Most companies choose the easier path. They build where the infrastructure already exists.

We chose the North.

The Decision

Primer exists because someone has to build what isn't there.

We're starting from zero. No listings to aggregate. No database to pull from. Every property we list, we visit. Every landlord we feature, we meet. Every mover and handyman we recommend, we vet.

It's manual work. It's slow. It doesn't scale quickly.

But it's the only way to build something real.

The Approach

Step by step. One neighborhood at a time.

We begin in one community. Our team goes out. We knock on doors. We talk to landlords. We photograph rooms. We ask questions that matter: Is the price real? Any hidden fees? Generator? Water? Who actually owns this place?

For movers and handymen, we do the same. We show up. We ask for references. We confirm skills. We agree on pricing before any job starts.

When we're done, for the first time in that neighborhood, there will be a small, trusted list of homes and service providers that people can actually rely on.

Then we move to the next neighborhood. Then the next. Then the next.

The Long Game

Primer isn't built for quick growth or viral metrics. It's built for the North.

We'll keep going until every major city in Northern Nigeria has what the South has had for years: a rental platform that actually works.

Until students can find homes without getting scammed. Until young professionals can book movers who show up and stick to the price. Until families can call a handyman and know they'll come.

That's the mission.

The Invitation

If this resonates with you—if you see the gap and want to help close it—we'd love to have you.

We need verifiers who aren't afraid of legwork. Engineers who want to build something meaningful. Movers and handymen who take pride in their work. And believers who understand that the hardest things are often the most worth building.

Primer is the first step.

Let's build.

— Alamin Ahmed, Founder
